source_name: Code4rena Angle Findings and Analysis Report (2023-06)
finding_title: Poor detection of disputed trees allows claiming tokens from a disputed tree
summary: Users can claim rewards from a Merkle tree that’s being disputed. This can potentially lead to loss of funds since a malicious trusted EOA can claim funds from a malicious tree while it’s being disputed.
root_cause: Merkle claim logic does not block claims from a currently disputed tree, allowing funds to be claimed before dispute resolution
broken_invariants: the reward distribution path must not allow the condition described by "Poor detection of disputed trees allows claiming tokens from a disputed tree" to change user balances, collateralization, or protocol availability unexpectedly
reward and distribution accounting must preserve each participant’s accrued value across claims, transfers, and configuration changes
state changes must remain internally consistent across all affected accounting buckets and external integrations
attack_preconditions: the affected component is deployed or configured in the way described by the final report
the report-described user, keeper, vault, connector, oracle, or liquidation flow is reachable in the audited code version
attacker_capability: external user, keeper, liquidator, vault participant, or protocol-integrated actor able to trigger the affected flow
trigger_actions: execute the report-described function sequence or state transition
observe mispricing, accounting drift, value loss, fund lock, or denial of service

impact.description: Users can claim rewards from a Merkle tree that’s being disputed. This can potentially lead to loss of funds since a malicious trusted EOA can claim funds from a malicious tree while it’s being disputed.

why_not_higher: already classified as High in the final C4 report
why_not_lower: included as a validated High/Medium C4 finding with public report evidence and affected code/finding references
common_false_positive_angles: verify the same component and configuration are in scope
check whether the issue is reachable by an untrusted or economically motivated actor
confirm the impact is more than a view-only mismatch, dust loss, or privileged-only misconfiguration
mitigations: When the disputer address is set (after a call to disputeTree), consider treating the current tree as disputed, no matter whether the dispute period has passed or not. E.g. consider these changes: diff --git a/contracts/Distributor.sol b/contracts/Distributor.sol index bc4e49f..8fb6a4c 100644 --- a/contracts/Distributor.sol +++ b/contracts/Distributor.sol @@ -197,7 +197,7 @@ contract Distributor is UUPSHelper { /// @notice Returns the MerkleRoot that is currently live for the contract function getMerkleRoot() public view returns (bytes32) { - if (block.timestamp >= endOfDisputePeriod) return tree.merkleRoot; + if (block.timestamp >= endOfDisputePeriod && disputer == address(0)) return tree.merkleRoot; else return lastTree.merkleRoot; }
